steps.title: Determine what the blank must mean
steps.content: The sentence argues that merely counting patents ______ the degree of innovation because truly novel patents are vastly outnumbered by nonnovel ones. A raw count makes innovation look different from what it really is, so the blank must mean gives a false picture of or misrepresents.

steps.title: Substitute each choice and eliminate
steps.content:
Test each word in the blank to see which fits the meaning of ‘gives a false impression of’:
  • Choice A — belies: Means contradicts or gives a false impression of — a count dominated by nonnovel patents belies (misrepresents) the true level of innovation. ✓
  • Choice B — portends: Means foretells or signals a future event — a patent count is not predicting anything; it is distorting a current picture. ✗
  • Choice C — thwarts: Means prevents or blocks — a patent count does not stop innovation from occurring; it just misrepresents its extent. ✗
  • Choice D — maligns: Means speaks badly of or slanders — a patent count does not criticize innovation; it distorts understanding of it. ✗
That is the correct answer.
